text: Millie Bobby Brown says that when she worked with Henry Cavill on Enola Holmes, he set very clear boundaries because she was only 15 at the time and he wanted to keep their relationship strictly professional.  \nIn an interview, Millie explained that Cavill had “terms and conditions,” joking that whenever she tried to ask about his personal life he would quickly shut it down with a simple “Millie, no,” to which she would reply “Understood.” She said she actually respected that a lot, describing him as someone who is naturally more private and reserved.  \nShe also noted that their dynamic felt more “adult” and professional compared to the playful friendships she shares with her Stranger Things castmates, emphasizing that Cavill’s approach helped maintain a respectful and appropriate working environment while they filmed together.
author: @SceneinCinema
why: 52k likes + 20M+ views — a nostalgic on-set story went massively viral, keeping the older Netflix film in active conversation.

text: With a roughly $20M budget, Boots Riley’s #ILoveBoosters is the most expensive film Neon has ever made, and its biggest swing as a company.  \n“On this movie specifically,” says Neon CEO Tom Quinn, “it felt that we were living up to the mission we represent: a safe harbor for filmmakers who have a very clear point of view.”  \nRead the full cover story:
